metadata.raw: {"ID":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/id/ssi/2025/406","TITLE":"The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025","LINK (SELF)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/id/ssi/2025/406","LINK":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made","LINK (XML)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.xml","LINK (RDF/XML)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.rdf","LINK (AKN)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.akn","LINK (HTML snippet)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.xht","LINK (Website (XHTML) Default View)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.htm","LINK (PDF)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/made/data.pdf","LINK (Table of Contents)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ssi/2025/406/contents/made","AUTHOR":"","UPDATED":"2025-12-18T16:39:05Z","PUBLISHED":"2025-12-18T16:33:56.999695Z","DocumentMainType":"ScottishStatutoryInstrument","YEAR":"2025","NUMBER":"406","ISBN":"'9780111064764'","AlternativeNumber":"","CreationDate":"2025-12-16","SUBJECT":"PUBLIC SERVICE PENSIONS","Supersedes":"","CATEGORY":"PUBLIC SERVICE PENSIONS","SUMMARY":"The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Order 2025 (“the Order”) amends the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/199) (“the Scheme”). The schedule of the Order makes various amendments to the Scheme relating to retained firefighters. These include amendments to introduce new awards which are payable following the death of a retained firefighter, to extend the deadline for their purchase of service during the extended limited period and to amend the eligibility criteria for some awards and conversions.","":""}

content: a standard member must be treated as special pensionable service,  (b) the member must continue to receive their ordinary or ill-health pension in respect of their pensionable service as a standard member, and  (c) the member must, in addition, receive their special member’s ordinary pension or ill-health pension in respect of their special pensionable service as a special member but the special member’s ordinary pension or ill-health pension in respect of their special pensionable service must be reduced in accordance with tables produced by the Scheme Actuary, so that the total amount of pension that the member receives in accordance with sub-paragraph (b) and this sub-paragraph does not exceed the amount they would have received as a special member if—  (i) they had not been an ordinary member of the Scheme, and  (ii) all of their service from the start of the mandatory special period to the date that the member’s special member’s ordinary pension or ill-health pension, as the case may be, becomes payable and which is eligible to be treated as special pensionable service, was treated as special pensionable service.  Converting membership from standard membership to special membership – additional provisions 18B. — (1) This rule applies to a person to whom rule 17 or 18 applies and who is—  (a) a special deferred member,  (b) a special pensioner member who was previously a special deferred member, or  (c) in receipt of a deferred pension under rule 5 of Part 3.  (2) Within two months of 21 February 2026 the relevant authority must use reasonable endeavours to notify the person of their eligibility to apply to convert their membership in accordance with rule 17 or 18, as applicable.  (3) A person to whom this rule applies—  (a) does not need to make an application under rule 17(2) or 18(2) at the same time as they make an application under rule 5A(5) or 5B(9) of Part 11,  (b) does not need to make an election under rule 17(5) or 18(5) at the same time as they make their election under rule 6A of Part 11,  (c) must not make an election under rule 17(5) or 18(5) more than three months after receiving the statements referred to in rule 17(4) or 18(4), and  (d) must not, in any event, make an election under rule 17(5) or 18(5) after 17 September 2026.  (4) The authority must provide the statements referred to in rule 17(4) or 18(4) within three months of receiving an application under rule 17(2) or 18(2), as applicable.  (5) Where a person did not receive a notification from the authority under paragraph (2), despite the authority using reasonable endeavours to notify them as required by that paragraph, an election under rule 17(5) or 18(5), as applicable, may be made after 17 September 2026. ” .  6. In Part 14 (miscellaneous provisions), after rule 6 (death on or before 31st March 2007 of retained or volunteer firefighter employed before 6th April 2006), insert— Retained firefighter opt-out member 7. — (1) This rule applies to a person who—  (a) is a retained firefighter opt-out member or the spouse, civil partner or child of a retained firefighter opt-out member who is deceased, and  (b) before 21 February 2026—  (i) received an award, benefit, pension or any other payment under this Scheme or,  (ii) in respect of a person who is a retained firefighter opt-out member, purchased or converted service under this Scheme.  (2) Subject to paragraph (5), and notwithstanding any provision of this Scheme, a person to whom this rule applies may apply to the authority for an additional entitlement.  (3) The additional entitlement must represent the period of the extended limited period during which the member or deceased member ceased to be a member of this Scheme.  (4) Within two months of 21 February 2026, the authority must use reasonable endeavours to notify the person of their eligibility for an additional ent
